name: Pickled Chilies
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a skillet. Add washed and dried chilies and cook until skins blister.
2. Remove the chilies with a slotted spoon and add carrots and onion. Cook until tender/crisp.
3. Pack the peeled chilies into hot, clean pint canning jars in standing position.
4. Season each jar with about 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon salt to taste.
5. Add 2 garlic cloves, carrot slices (4-6), and onion slices (2) to each jar.
6. Mix equal parts vinegar and water in a pot until boiling. Pour the mixture over the chilies, leaving at least 1 inch of headspace in each jar.
7. Remove air bubbles by sliding a clean knife around the inside and middle of each filled jar.
8. Add olive oil to within 1/2 inch of the rim of each jar.
9. Secure lids and caps tightly.
10. Process jars in a hot water bath for 15 minutes.
11. Remove jars and place on towels away from drafts until cool.
12. Let stand 30-45 minutes before sealing.
13. Check seals by pressing them; sealed lids will not flex.
14. Store sealed jars in the pantry or under a sink in a cool, dark place. Unsealed jars should be refrigerated.
